Alright everybody, welcome back to the program. Before we get too deep, I just want to give you a really quick lay of the land so that you can really maximize your time, your energy, and results inside this track. So the first thing I want to point out to you is that you really should be applying this program with an active project. I really don't want you just taking a bunch of notes and theories that sound good on paper, but don't move the needle in any kind of real way. So it is important that you do have a client's product or your own product to use this material on. With that said, here's how things are going to go down. So 10 Excel's pages track 2, your roadmap is as follows. We're going to start with module 1, where you're really going to get grounded on the coaching, the conversion ethos as it relates to sales pages. In module 2, we're going to do our deep dive into our avatar. You're going to learn a new system called Coach Mining, and you're going to learn about the launch Bible, which is really the synthesis of all the data you collect. And then from there, you're going to go into modules 3 through 6, which are really our workshop, our writing sessions, and they all have to do with establishing the gap between where your prospect is and where they want to be, exploring what is happening between that gap, inviting them to exit it, and then giving them a little nudge with a little more urgency to escape it. So to just get a little more deeper, part 1 is really going to be the funnel foundations and the message mining. In module 1, as I mentioned, you're going to get grounded in the coaching, the conversion ethos. It means why that is a more natural, intuitive framework for non-copyrighters and copywriters alike, and you're going to be introduced to the 15-point sales page in a day method, which is really the basis of the entire track. From there, module 2 is going to have the 3D Avatar deep dive, which is a series of 67 questions that I use to begin any project, whether I'm writing email copy or landing pages or sales pages, this is what I use to start every project to get super grounded and steeped in who I'm communicating to. At the end of that, we're going to have the 5 advanced empathy visualizations that really give you a chance to bring your prospect to life in a very vivid way, make them real in your mind so that when you are writing copy, you feel like you're actually communicating to a real person instead of having this one-way conversation with pretty much a blinking cursor. Then there's going to be the process that I call coach mining, which is about how to take your coaching calls, your enrollment conversations, or even if you're in SaaS, your product demos, and extract the information you gather from there and use that as your research. Use that as the place where you dig for deeper insights and use that to coach the conversion at scale on your sales page. Then finally, we're going to take all this information that you've gathered through these different tools and synthesize them into the launch Bible. The launch Bible is a paid deliverable if you're a copywriter. It could be a service in of itself as you really give your client a full understanding of who their prospect is, what they want, how they're evolving, what the transformation is that you're coaching them into, and how their product fits into that whole process. At this point, you'll have taken those raw materials you've gathered and really start implementing them into the 15 sections that make up the sales page in a day theme plate. The first thing I want you to know is that each module can be seen as a writing session. Each writing session should take you roughly four to five hours to go through the lesson, look at the examples where you need and really execute upon what you're learning. If we give five hours for four writing workshops, we're really looking at about 20 hours of writing. That's the sales page in a day title. That is a super ambitious day. I don't recommend you do write it all in a day. I have for circumstances that I probably shouldn't have allowed it to get to, but my recommendation would be to spread that out across five days. Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, write one of these sections per day and leave a day for really editing. That said, that is the goal, really to take about a week in the research phase in part one and another week in the workshop writing phase for two weeks, which are done at more of a moderate, a consistent, but more of a moderate pace. With that said, this is how each module breaks down a little bit further. The first workshop session is all about establishing the gap, giving a clear, vivid picture of where your prospect is as it relates to the problem you solve, how that's showing up for them and all other stakeholders in their life in a specific way, then contrasting that with how things should be. Now notice I said should and not could, and there's a really good reason for that, which we'll explore more deeply when we get to module three. We're also going to be using this first section to generate enough will, desire, motivation, and perceived stakes for your prospect to invest the attention and energy necessary to explore it in the next part of your sales page. A really important thing to remember here is that most people reading your sales page will be in a state of inertia or have at least a backseat passenger in the form of their lizard brain who just dampens any desire for immediate change or transformation. The first goal is to overcome that inertia, to create energy, to invite resourcefulness and promise a really compelling reward for continuing to read. What we're ultimately doing here in module three is creating the container to coach the transformation between point A and point buyer. Now in module four, after we've established the gap, our goal is to explore it more intimately with our prospect. This is where we give them the gift, and once again, the word the gift of understanding around why things have gone a certain way. Diagnosing the symptoms they're feeling and offering them the gift of an emotionally soothing and intellectually satisfying why it wasn't your fault. Before, later filling that chasm with the new empowering beliefs which if followed or lived by will deliver the outcome they desire, the one that's eluded them for so long. So these agreements or belief shifts will be the raw materials or building blocks of the bridge that brings them to the other side of the gap. They're called blue ocean because your solution uniquely satisfies the conditions you set up earlier. There is literally no other product or service that will be able to satisfy those new beliefs or agreements that you just had your prospect buy into. So we'll be going much deeper into how to engineer this later, but for now just understand that your prospect's ability to act and respond, which really is the definition of direct response, is a function of how much energy can be liberated from past failures and channeled into present action. Let me just say that one more time. Your prospect's ability to respond is a function of how much energy can be liberated from the past and channeled to the present. So in these five sections we'll ramp up their responsiveness by illuminating the reason for those failures, inviting self-forgiveness and leading them into a new set of beliefs that serve the outcome they want and will then fasten those new beliefs through testimonials and our bio which just builds up the trust that A we can deliver it and B that they can succeed with our help. Now module five will be all about inviting your prospect to exit the gap. So at this point you've made the gap such an undesirable place and more importantly an unnecessary place to remain by shifting their beliefs and ideas around why they're stuck there. By now remaining stuck is a choice and they can no longer be a victim of circumstance. They're empowered to accept an invitation and it's your job to be so kind as to extend that invitation and show them exactly what that looks like in a tangible, exciting way while removing as much risk as possible to cross that chasm with you. Now finally in module six this will be all about effectively and ethically ramping up the urgency and giving your prospect a little more help and reassurance to escape that gap. So whereas in module five it's more of this invitation to exit calmly showing them that escape door. Module six carries a little bit more urgency. Here we're really going to illuminate the stakes and grab them by that hand and move them out of that gap once and for all. In this module to really do that you'll learn the three primary closes that I use in every sales page as well as two advanced bonus closes that give your prospect a little more of that loving nudge to finally take action. At the end of the day they're really all supportive of coaching decisiveness, ramping up your prospect's ability to make a decision. Most conversions don't fail because people say no but because people don't make a decision at all and that's what these closes are really designed to do just on different levels. The upgraded FAQ works more on a logical rational level, the perfect for you if close is more on a self identification level and creating this almost existential crisis and misalignment with staying stuck. And finally the final confident appeal really makes them feel safe in following your lead as they escape. You can't expect your prospect to buy with total confidence and 100% all in this and it's kind of an unfair expectation we place on our prospects. Instead we want them to borrow our confidence to really latch onto that and when people are in a stuck place they need that borrowed confidence in order to move forward and that's why we really wrap this up with that final confident appeal and close the question loop we opened earlier. With all that said feel free to take a short break, a little stretch and then meet me in module one where you'll get introduced or reacquainted to the coaching the conversion model as it pertains to sales pages and a more complete rundown of the 15 point sales page in a day method. I'll see you there.
